title: Jahmai Jones hits memorable first home run for Red Sox
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Jahmai Jones hit a two-run home run for the Red Sox against the Rays in his second at-bat for Boston.
Jones' home run tied the game 2-2, providing a memorable moment for fans at Fenway Park.
Acquired from the Tigers, Jones has struggled with a .135 average but hit 23 RBIs and 7 home runs in 2025.

title: Wilyer Abreu joins elite Red Sox company following his back-to-back multi-HR games
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Wilyer Abreu hit four home runs in consecutive games against the Tampa Bay Rays at Fenway Park.
Abreu is the first Red Sox player to achieve back-to-back multi-home run games since Mookie Betts in 2016.
Boston Red Sox improved their record over .500 with Abreu's performance, aiming for a 13th consecutive win.

title: Red Sox extend season-altering win streak to 12 games in epic comeback fashion
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Boston Red Sox extended their win streak to 12 games with a comeback victory over the Tampa Bay Rays.
Wilyer Abreu hit two home runs, becoming the first Red Sox player with back-to-back multi-homer games since 2016.
The Red Sox hold the final American League wild-card spot, leading the Mariners by 1.5 games and Orioles by 2 games.

title: Report: LeBron James ‘hates’ Celtics, would rank them last on his list of teams to join
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: LeBron James reportedly ranks Boston Celtics last on his list of potential NBA teams to join.
ESPN's Brian Windhorst reported that James 'hates' Boston, citing past rivalries and fan incidents.
James is considering joining the Heat, Cavaliers, or Warriors for his final NBA season.

title: Tom Brady busy at Fanatics Fest, from a face slap to showing off his arm
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Tom Brady slapped Logan Paul at the Fanatics Fest NYC 2026, leading to a viral moment.
The slap followed a playful feud between Brady and Paul over their flag football match in March.
Brady also showcased his arm by accurately throwing a baseball and participated in other sports activities at the event.

title: 4 charged in connection with alleged kidnapping, beating near Nantucket park
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Four men from Nantucket face kidnapping and assault charges for allegedly attacking a 28-year-old near a playground.
The suspects, identified as Calray Brown, Dijon Hazel, Newton Lawrence, and Alvin Barnett, allegedly restrained and beat the victim.
Video evidence reportedly shows the men attacking the victim; they were released on bail and scheduled for arraignment Monday.

title: 2 arrested at Logan Airport as ICE activity ramps up
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Maria Rosales and David Ardila were arrested at Logan Airport and are currently detained in Massachusetts.
ICE arrested Rosales, protected under DACA, and Ardila, an asylum seeker, for visa and removal order issues.
Rosales' and Ardila's attorney reports increased immigration-related arrests at Logan, citing delays in legal applications.

title: 5 movies to watch after ‘The Odyssey’
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: "Interstellar" (2014) parallels "The Odyssey" with its time-bending quest by a NASA pilot, available on Hulu and Paramount+.
"Lawrence of Arabia" (1962) shares thematic elements with "The Odyssey," focusing on struggles similar to those of Odysseus.
"O Brother, Where Art Thou?" (2000) humorously adapts Homer's tale featuring elements like sirens and a cyclops, available on the Criterion Channel.

title: Formidable Red Sox extend win streak to 11 games with doubleheader sweep of Rays
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox achieve longest win streak since 2016, sweeping Rays in doubleheader, reaching 11 consecutive victories.
Boston's diverse offensive effort includes standout performances by Jarren Duran, Carlos Narvaez, and Masataka Yoshida.
Win places Red Sox at .500 for first time since March, holding AL's third wild-card spot above Mariners and Twins.

title: Chad Tracy offers updates on several injured Red Sox pitchers
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Ranger Suarez and Connelly Early are progressing well in recovery and may return to the Red Sox soon.
Kutter Crawford, Tanner Houck, and Johan Oviedo are making strides in rehabilitation with potential returns this season.
No recovery updates for Roman Anthony, Garrett Crochet, and others as they remain inactive without a return timetable.
